GitHub nav

htmx-extensions/server-sent-events

The Server-Sent Events extension lets you wire up htmx to consume an SSE endpoint, swap incoming events into the DOM, and reconnect automatically when the connection drops. It is the simplest way to add real-time updates to an htmx app without introducing a JavaScript framework or a websocket dependency.

Install

<script src="https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2"></script>
<script src="https://unpkg.com/htmx.org/dist/ext/server-sent-events.js"></script>

Usage

Mark a container with the sse-connect attribute pointing at your SSE endpoint, then use sse-swap on the children that should be updated:

<div hx-ext="sse" sse-connect="/events">
  <div sse-swap="message">
    waiting...
  </div>
</div>

When the server emits an event named message, the inner div is replaced. The default swap is innerHTML; pass hx-swap="outerHTML" to swap the element itself.

Server contract

Your server must respond with Content-Type: text/event-stream and flush messages in the standard SSE format: lines beginning with data:, optionally with a event: prefix to name the event, and a blank line between messages.

Reconnection

If the connection drops, the extension reconnects automatically with exponential backoff (starting at 1 second, capped at 30 seconds). You can disable this with sse-reconnect="false".

Authentication

Cookies are sent with the SSE request by default. For token-based auth, you can intercept the connection event and add a header — but be aware that EventSource has limited header support in browsers. Most production deployments use cookies + same-origin or a short-lived signed token in the URL.

License

BSD-2-Clause.
